# TinyUa

> A lightweight, from-scratch OPC UA client stack for .NET 8. Core libraries have zero dependency on the OPC Foundation SDK.

## Intro

TinyUa implements the full OPC UA binary protocol from scratch — binary encoding, security policies (4 policies + 3 token types), transport layer with message chunking, and a high-level client with Fluent API. It targets .NET 8 and uses only `System.Security.Cryptography` for all cryptographic operations. The OPC Foundation SDK (`OPCFoundation.NetStandard.Opc.Ua`) is only used in test and example projects for reference comparisons.

## Quick Start

```bash
dotnet build TinyUa.sln
dotnet run --project TinyUa.Example -- selftest
```

Minimal code:
```csharp
var client = await UaClient.ConnectTo("opc.tcp://server:4840")
    .WithSecurity("Basic256Sha256")
    .WithUserName("user", "pass")
    .BuildAndRunAsync();

var value = await client.ReadAsync("ns=2;s=Temperature");
Console.WriteLine(value);
```

## API

**Fluent Builder:**
- `UaClient.ConnectTo(url)` — start builder
- `.WithSecurity("Basic256Sha256")` — set security policy (short name)
- `.WithSecurityMode(MessageSecurityMode.SignAndEncrypt)`
- `.WithUserName(user, pass)` — username/password authentication
- `.WithAppName("MyApp")` — set application name
- `.EnableLog(ILogger)` / `.EnableLogFile(path)` — logging
- `.BuildAndRunAsync()` — connect and create session

**Services:**
- `ReadAsync(nodeId)` — read a single node value
- `ReadAsync(nodeId[])` — **batch read** multiple nodes in one request (much faster for many variables)
- `ReadValueAsync<T>(nodeId)` — typed single-node read (returns `T`)
- `WriteAsync(nodeId, value)` — write a single node value
- `WriteAsync(WriteValue[])` — **batch write** multiple values in one request (much faster for many variables)
- `BrowseAsync(nodeId)` — browse children
- `BrowseNextAsync(continuationPoint)` — continue browse
- `CreateSubscriptionAsync(interval)` — create subscription, returns IDisposable

**Configuration (`UaClientOptions`):**
- `Security.Policy` — "None", "Basic256Sha256", "Aes128Sha256RsaOaep", "Aes256Sha256RsaPss"
- `Security.Mode` — None, Sign, SignAndEncrypt
- `Security.UserIdentity` — Anonymous, UserName, X509 certificate
- `Security.Certificate.AutoDiscoverServerCertificate` — auto-discover server cert via GetEndpoints
- `Security.Certificate.AutoAcceptServerCertificate` — auto-trust server cert
- `ReconnectMaxRetries` / `ReconnectInitialDelayMs` / `ReconnectMaxDelayMs` — exponential backoff
- `Timeout` — request timeout in ms
- `ErrorMode` — Throw or ReturnNull on error

## Security

| Policy | Key Exchange | Symmetric Crypto | Asymmetric Crypto |
|--------|-------------|------------------|-------------------|
| None | — | — | — |
| Basic256Sha256 | RSA-OAEP-SHA256 | AES-256-CBC + HMAC-SHA256 | RSA-SHA256 |
| Aes128Sha256RsaOaep | RSA-OAEP-SHA256 | AES-128-CBC + HMAC-SHA256 | RSA-SHA256 |
| Aes256Sha256RsaPss | RSA-OAEP-SHA256 (PSS) | AES-256-CBC + HMAC-SHA256 | RSA-PSS-SHA256 |

Token types: Anonymous, UserName, X509 Certificate

## Project Structure

| Project | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| TinyUa.Core | Binary encoding, OPC UA types, security policies, logging |
| TinyUa.Transport | Message chunking (OPN/MSG), secure channel, key derivation |
| TinyUa.Client | Fluent API, connection management, services, subscriptions |
| TinyUa.Example | Console self-test app with in-process server |
| TinyUa.Explorer | WPF GUI browser with security endpoint discovery |
| TinyUa.Benchmarks | Performance and crypto primitive benchmarks |
| TinyUa.Client.Tests | xUnit client tests |
| TinyUa.Security.Tests | xUnit security integration tests |
